title: Settings: disk-usage progress bar for the selected DC's network volume (used / total GB) â€” needs a wrapper /wrapper/disk endpoint; RunPod API has no used-bytes
description:
GOAL (user, 2026-07-01): add a progress bar to the RunPod section of Settings showing how much disk space is used on the network volume for the currently selected data center (used GB / total GB).

WHAT ALREADY EXISTS (investigated read-only 2026-07-01):
- DENOMINATOR (total): `vol.size` (configured quota GB) from REST GET /networkvolumes (runpodRemote.js listVolumes ~line 224). Already shown in the volume badge at MpiSettings.js _renderRunpodVolume ~line 1478/1531 ('âœ“ name Â· {size} GB Â· Ready').
- PROGRESS BAR PRIMITIVE: MpiProgressBar already exists (js/components/Primitives/MpiProgressBar/MpiProgressBar.js). Static usage: mount({min:0,max:totalGb,value:usedGb,info:'{value} / N GB used',variant:'primary',interactive:false,handle:false}); has setValueQuiet(v) for poll updates. REUSE it â€” do NOT build a new bar.
- DC selection state: state.runpodConfig.datacenter (js/state.js ~line 130, persisted via Storage.setRunpodConfig).
- Mount slot: inside the mpi-settings__form-group wrapping #mpiSettingsRunpodVolumeSlot (MpiSettings.js ~160-165), right after the existing volume badge in _renderRunpodVolume().

HARD BLOCKER (the NUMERATOR does not exist server-side):
- RunPod REST GET /networkvolumes returns only the configured `size` (total quota) â€” NEVER live used bytes. Documented already at downloadManager.js:1178-1185.
- RunPod GraphQL (dataCenters, gpuTypes) has NO disk fields.
- The in-Pod wrapper GET /wrapper/stats reports RAM + VRAM ONLY (remoteProxy.js /remote/pod/stats ~1084-1252). A `disk` block was shipped in image v0.4.5 then REVERTED (remoteProxy.js:59-62) because statvfs reads the multi-PB container overlay, NOT the volume quota â€” useless.
- => Used-bytes is only measurable by mounting the volume and MEASURING it (du -sb on the mount), which only a RUNNING Pod can do. So a truthful bar can only reflect the volume of the DC whose Pod you're CONNECTED to; an idle/unconnected DC has no live reader.

SPIKE DONE 2026-07-01 (live API probe, research/api-probe-2026-07-01.md): RunPod exposes NO used-bytes â€” CONFIRMED DEAD. REST /networkvolumes returns only {id,name,size,dataCenterId}; GraphQL NetworkVolume type REJECTS `used`/`usedBytes`/`consumedBytes`/`currentPerGBUsage` ('Cannot query field'). So there is NO API path to used bytes and NO zero-pod option. The wrapper `du` path below is the ONLY way â†’ the bar is CONNECTED-POD-ONLY (works for the DC whose Pod is currently connected; idle/selected DC = total-only badge). This is now settled, not a decision.

WRAPPER PATH (the only path): add GET /wrapper/disk to the Pod wrapper (mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/wrapper.py) that runs `du -sb /workspace` (NOT statvfs â€” that was the reverted mistake) and returns used bytes; app-side add /remote/pod/disk route (parallel to /remote/pod/stats in remoteProxy.js) + poll; denominator from vol.size. Requires a wrapper version bump + R2 publish via publish-runtime.sh (NO full image rebuild). CONSTRAINT: only works while CONNECTED to a Pod that has the volume mounted â€” an idle selected DC shows total-only (existing badge). MUST WORK ON BOTH POD TYPES (user, 2026-07-01): the wrapper endpoint + the app bar must fire for a GPU pod connection AND a CPU download pod â€” both mount the volume at /workspace, so `du /workspace` is engine-agnostic. Do NOT scope this to CPU-only. The wrapper endpoint lives in the shared wrapper (both the GPU image and the -cpu image build from it), and the app-side /remote/pod/disk + bar are gated only on 'a pod is connected', not on pod type.

PRODUCT DECISION (settled by the probe + user): 'currently selected DC' CANNOT work when not connected â€” RunPod exposes no used-bytes (proven dead). The bar is CONNECTED-POD-ONLY and that's ACCEPTED (user 2026-07-01). It must show on BOTH a GPU pod and a CPU download pod (whichever is connected). The CPU download pod is where fullness matters MOST (download/upload workflow) but the bar is NOT limited to it. Idle/selected-but-unconnected DC keeps the existing total-only badge.

FILES: routes/runpodRemote.js (listVolumes ~224), routes/remoteProxy.js (/remote/pod/stats ~1084, add /remote/pod/disk), js/components/Compounds/LandingPages/MpiSettings/MpiSettings.js (_renderRunpodVolume ~1478), js/components/Primitives/MpiProgressBar/MpiProgressBar.js (reuse), mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/wrapper.py (add /wrapper/disk if wrapper path). Related dead-end research: MPI-100 (statvfs revert).

BRANCH: RunPod. Spike the RunPod API for a used-bytes field BEFORE scoping the wrapper bump.
